creator: gavin@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->feedback

To submitter: Firstly, I could do with some more details about how exactly 
things are crashing for you.  With amd64, what exactly happened when it 
"crashed at the start"?  How early in the boot process do you mean? 

On i386, do you mean that the machine hung at the "waiting 5 seconds" message? 
How long did you wait?  Did the caps-lock key work?  If you boot with verbose 
logging (one of the options on the boot menu) do you get any messages printed 
during the hang?  If the machine has a floppy drive, was it's activity LED lit? 

Secondly, have you tried 7.0-RELEASE?  It's possible that these problems have 
already been fixed, and for a new server, starting with 7.0 is probably a good 
idea anyway.  If you do try 7.0, I'd be interested to know the results from 
both the amd64 and the i386 versions. 

As an aside, what have you read on the Internet that has put you off 
using amd64 with FreeBSD 6.3?
